summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that uploaded files can be downloaded by unauthenticated users, demonstrating missing file download access control in Parse Server.
expected_security_impact: File uploaded by authenticated user should not be accessible via unauthenticated GET request to file URL. Expected 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden response.
actual_observed_result: File was successfully downloaded by unauthenticated request, returning the exact file content without any authentication check. This confirms the vulnerability that anyone with a file URL can access uploaded files.
verification_method: Upload file as authenticated user, extract file URL, then attempt download without session token. Compare downloaded content with uploaded content. If content matches without auth, vulnerability is confirmed.
evidence_produced_by_run: File uploaded successfully with session token
Unauthenticated GET request returned exact file content
File download handler does not check authentication
FilesController getHandler only validates appId, not user session

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_e8d9a6dc/repo and builds Docker image
